title: The ethical significance of user-control in AI-driven speech-BCIs: a narrative review.

assets.abstract: AI-driven brain-computed interfaces aimed at restoring speech for individuals living with locked-in-syndrome are paired with ethical implications for user's autonomy, privacy and responsibility. Embedding options for sufficient levels of user-control in speech-BCI design has been proposed to mitigate these ethical challenges. However, how user-control in speech-BCIs is conceptualized and how it relates to these ethical challenges is underdetermined. In this narrative literature review, we aim to

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Inner speech, mantra meditation, focused attention, semantic cognition, verbal repetition.
